sanskrit: एतन्मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुमर्हस्यशेषतः । त्वदन्यः संशयस्यास्य छेत्ता न ह्युपपद्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: हे कृष्ण मेरे इस सन्देहका सर्वथा छेदन करनेके लिये आप ही योग्य हैं क्योंकि इस संशयका छेदन करनेवाला आपके सिवाय दूसरा कोई हो नहीं सकता ॥
englishTranslation: O Krishna, only you can fully cut away this doubt. No one else is able to remove it.
essence: Only clear seeing can end confusion completely.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna asks Krishna to remove his doubt completely, saying no one else can resolve it.
simpleInsight: Some questions need a teacher who can see the whole path, not just one answer.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is shaken by a deeper fear than war itself: what happens to sincere practice that falters? After asking Krishna about the fate of such a person, he now admits that only Krishna can end this doubt fully.
modernRelevance: You keep circling the same decision at 1 a.m., reading opinions, still unsure. At some point, you need someone who can see the whole pattern and name the real issue.
translationLiteral: एतत् (this) / मे (my) / संशयम् (doubt) / कृष्ण (O Krishna) / छेत्तुम् (to cut) / अर्हसि (you are able) / अशेषतः (completely) / त्वदन्यः (other than you) / संशयस्य (of the doubt) / अस्य (of this) / छेत्ता (cutter) / न (not) / हि (indeed) / उपपद्यते (is possible)
insight: It shifts the mind from self-argument to seeking clear guidance from someone who sees beyond confusion.
